question: In Adobe Character Animator, how do I pin a character's legs in place during rigging?
answer: Click the 'Rig' tab, select the 'Frontal' layer in the 'Properties' panel under 'body', then drag a yellow line from the bottom of the character's body to pin the legs.
keypoints: The user clicks on the 'Rig' tab located at the top of the Character Animator interface.
From the 'Properties' panel, the user selects the 'Frontal' layer under the 'body' section.
The cursor drags a yellow line from the bottom of the character's body to pin it in place, ensuring the legs remain fixed during animation.

question: In Adobe Character Animator, what steps are needed to change a layer's movement setting to 'Free'?
answer: Click the 'Rig' tab, select the 'Frontal' layer in the left-side panel, then drag the 'Draggable' option from 'Fixed' to 'Free' in the right-side 'Properties' section.
keypoints: The user clicks on the 'Rig' tab located in the top menu bar of the Adobe Character Animator interface.
The user selects the 'Frontal' layer within the character's body structure in the left-side panel, highlighting it for manipulation.
The cursor drags the 'Draggable' option from 'Fixed' to 'Free' under the 'Properties' section on the right side of the screen, changing the movement settings of the selected layer.
